title: node:test, Original Feed Fixtures, Property Cases, and Compile-Negative Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for node:test, original feed fixtures, property cases, and compile-negative evidence and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for layered TypeScript feed evidence suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, entity, depth, date, identity, revision, malformed, and oversized fixtures with exact expectations.
Test URL, discovery, parser options, unknown validation, normalization, schedule, cursor, state, and policy with injected clocks and ports.
Control deferred settlement, abort order, time, lease expiry, pool error, listener cleanup, and unhandled rejection evidence deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
